standard: Tôi người An Giang này. Thấy bạn bốc phét quá nên mới nói thôi.
A: I'm from An Giang. I only said this because I saw you bragging too much.
B: I'm from An Giang. I'm only saying this because I see you're talking nonsense.
C: I'm from An Giang. I just had to speak up because you're exaggerating way too much.

standard: Tình hình sao rồi? Không đi tránh ở đâu à?
A: How are things? Aren't you going to hide anywhere?
B: How is the situation? Aren't you going somewhere to avoid it?
C: How's it going? Not going to shelter somewhere?

standard: Tại sao người ta chia sẻ sự học ngu của tao vậy?
A: Why are people sharing my poor academic performance?
B: Why are people sharing how stupid I am?
C: Why do people share about my being bad at studying?

standard: Trầu chưa chắc đã sang. Phải không bạn?
A: Chewing betel nut doesn't necessarily mean you're sophisticated. Isn't that right?
B: Betel leaves aren't necessarily fancy. Right?
C: Trầu might not have arrived yet. Right?

standard: Ngay chợ có duy nhất 1 cô 5 bán bánh canh tép ngon tuyệt vời luôn.
A: Right in the market, there's only one lady selling shrimp noodle soup that's absolutely delicious.
B: There is only one lady, Co 5, at the market who sells absolutely wonderful shrimp thick noodle soup.
C: At this market, there's only one Aunt 5 who sells shrimp noodle soup, and it's absolutely delicious.

standard: Nghỉ hè lâu quá giờ dậy sớm chưa quen.
A: The summer break was so long, I'm not used to waking up early anymore.
B: Summer break was so long that I'm not used to waking up early yet.
C: Summer break was so long, I haven't gotten used to waking up early yet.

standard: Đã xác định không lấy vợ nữa, hỏi làm gì.
A: I've already decided not to get married again, so why ask?
B: I've already decided not to get married anymore, so why ask?
C: I've decided not to get married anymore, so what's there to do?

standard: Nước Cửa Lò chắc chưa trong xanh như này đâu bạn nhỉ?
A: The water in Cua Lo probably isn't as clear and blue as this, is it?
B: The water in Cua Lo probably isn't as clear and blue as this, right?
C: The water at Cửa Lò probably isn't this clear, is it?

standard: Nhanh làm gì mà khổ vậy không biết
A: Why rush when you can do it so badly?
B: I don't know why I'm rushing so much just to make things hard for myself.
C: Why do you rush like that and suffer for it, I don't understand

standard: Sao chúng ta không thấy gì nhỉ?
A: Why can't we see anything?
B: Why don't we see anything?
C: Why can't we see anything?

standard: ngon tuyệt vời luôn đấy chị.
A: It's absolutely delicious!
B: It's absolutely delicious, sister.
C: It's absolutely delicious and amazing, sis.

standard: Nguyễn Trúc chịu khó, đảm đang thì có chứ hiền lành thì không có đâu nhỉ
A: Nguyen Truc is hardworking and capable, but she's definitely not kind-hearted, is she?
B: Nguyễn Trúc is hardworking and capable, but she's definitely not gentle, right?
C: Nguyễn Trúc is hardworking and capable, that's for sure, but she's not gentle at all, is she?

standard: mùng 8 này chị về bên đấy này bé có vào được không?
A: My sister is going back there on the 8th; can the child come over?
B: I'm coming back there on the 8th; can the baby come in?
C: On this 8th, I'm going over there - can my little one come in?

standard: Ở bên đó thì sao mà biết được nhà như nào.
A: How would you know what the houses are like over there?
B: How would I know what the house is like from over there?
C: How would anyone know what the house is like from over there?

standard: về theo nghề mẹ có khi lại giàu.
A: Following in my mother's footsteps might even make me rich.
B: Following my mother's profession might actually make me rich.
C: Following our mother's profession might make us rich.

standard: Ông ấy chụp mà nhìn mặt ông ấy nản chưa!
A: Look how disappointed he looks in that photo!
B: He took the photo, but look at how discouraged his face looks!
C: He took (the photo) and looking at his face, isn't he discouraged yet!

standard: Cú này bưng đủ.
A: This one's got it all covered.
B: This hit took everything.
C: I've had enough.

standard: Hỏi ngớ ngẩn. Mày có biết không mà hỏi người khác.
A: What a silly question. Do you even know the answer yourself? Why are you asking someone else?
B: Silly question. Do you even know the answer, yet you're asking others?
C: What a silly question. Do you even know (the answer) that you have to ask others?

standard: Nước đó nghèo khủng khiếp có quả cam cũng bâu lại chia.
A: That country was terribly poor; they would even huddle together to share an orange.
B: That country is terribly poor; even if there is an orange, they all crowd around to share it.
C: That country is extremely poor, even when they have an orange, they still gather together to share it.

standard: mày làm gì xấu hổ vậy em
A: What are you doing that's so embarrassing, girl?
B: What are you doing that's so embarrassing?
C: What embarrassing thing did you do?

standard: Chưa biết chỗ này bạn ạ.
A: I'm not familiar with this place.
B: I don't know this place yet.
C: I don't know this place.

standard: Rồi quần áo khô chưa?
A: Are the clothes dry yet?
B: So, are the clothes dry yet?
C: Are the clothes dry yet?

standard: Thèm mà đụng vào bỏng miệng, ngày xưa đặt ống tre ăn không hết, giờ muốn bị tuyệt chủng.
A: It's so tempting, but touching it burns your mouth. Back in the day, we could eat it all in bamboo tubes, but now we're on the verge of extinction.
B: I crave it, but touching it burns my mouth; in the past, we used bamboo tubes and couldn't finish it all, and now it's on the verge of extinction.
C: I'm craving it but it touches my mouth sores, back in the day [we] bought bamboo tubes of it and couldn't finish eating, now I wish it would go extinct.

standard: ai bảo mày nhìn nó. Nhìn tao đỡ hơn này
A: Who told you to look at him? Look at me, it's better.
B: Who told you to look at them? Look at me instead, it's better.
C: Who told you to look at that? Look at me instead, it's better.

standard: Nói đi đôi với làm . Mà toàn thấy nói chứ không thấy làm đâu
A: Actions speak louder than words. But all I see is talk, not action.
B: Actions should match words. But all I see is talking, and no action.
C: Saying should go hand in hand with doing. But all I see is talk, no action at all.

standard: Sao vậy nhỉ?
A: Why is that?
B: I wonder why?
C: I wonder why?

standard: chúng mày qua đón là hết mình luôn chứ đùa.
A: When you guys come to pick me up, you'll go all out, no joke.
B: If you guys come pick me up, I'll give it my all, no joke.
C: You all come pick us up, and we'll go all out, no joke.

standard: hôm nào lên mẹ xong vào đây lang thang.
A: I'll come here to wander around after I've visited my mother someday.
B: Someday when I visit my mother, I'll come here to wander around.
C: Some day when I'm done, I'll come wander around here.

standard: Anh hay nói miệng vậy thôi. Dám làm gì!
A: He just talks big. He wouldn't dare do anything!
B: You always just talk like that. You wouldn't dare actually do it!
C: He always talks big like that, but wouldn't dare actually do anything!

standard: Không đi Huế thì đi đâu nữa.
A: If you don't go to Hue, where else would you go?
B: If we don't go to Hue, where else would we go?
C: If we're not going to Hue, where else is there to go?

standard: Hải Dương quê ta nay đã đẹp giàu.
A: Our hometown of Hai Duong is now beautiful and prosperous.
B: Our hometown of Hai Duong has now become beautiful and prosperous.
C: Hai Duong, our hometown, is now beautiful and prosperous.

standard: Thu nhập thế mà ngân hàng có mỗi 200 triệu?
A: With such a high income, why does the bank only have 200 million?
B: With that kind of income, you only have 200 million in the bank?
C: With that income, the bank only has 200 million?

standard: Chúng mày ghép ảnh rồi phán tào lao. Chứ tao đi mòn Tuyên Hoá có thấy đâu nào.
A: You guys are photoshopping pictures and making baseless claims. I've searched Tuyen Hoa extensively and haven't seen anything like it.
B: You guys photoshopped the images and then made nonsense claims. I've traveled all over Tuyen Hoa and haven't seen it anywhere.
C: You guys edited photos and then talked nonsense. I went all the way to Tuyên Hóa and didn't see any of that at all.

standard: sao giống thằng Phi già thế này.
A: Why does he look so much like that old Filipino guy?
B: Why does this look so much like old Phi?
C: It's so similar to thằng Phi già like this.

standard: ở An Giang xưa giờ chỉ biết gọi là bánh cống chứ bánh ống không biết.
A: In An Giang, people have always only known it as "banh cong," but they don't know what "banh ong" is.
B: In An Giang, since a long time ago, I've only known it as bánh cống; I don't know about bánh ống.
C: In An Giang, we only knew how to call it bánh cống, not bánh ống.

standard: dùng với bún nước lèo là tuyệt vời.
A: It's excellent when served with noodle soup.
B: It is wonderful when used with bún nước lèo.
C: It's wonderful with bún nước lèo.

standard: Chỗ nào Yên Thành thế này nhỉ?
A: Where in Yen Thanh is this place?
B: Where in Yen Thanh is this?
C: Which place is like this, Yên Thành?

standard: Có khi nào ông ấy lạc luôn trong xứ sở thần tiên luôn không?
A: Could he possibly get lost in Wonderland forever?
B: Is it possible that he got lost in Wonderland forever?
C: Could it be that he's gotten completely lost in wonderland or what?

standard: Hấn đảo chắc phải dẻo hơn tôi rang lạc.
A: The peanuts on the island must be chewier than the peanuts I roast.
B: His island must be more flexible than my roasted peanuts.
C: Hấn Đảo must be more bendy than my roasted peanuts.

standard: Thèm chứ bạn nhỉ, trời này có ăn thì tuyệt.
A: Of course I'm craving it, it would be perfect to eat something like this in this weather.
B: I'm craving it, aren't you? It would be wonderful to eat this in this weather.
C: I'm craving it, you know, with this weather it would be gorgeous if we could eat (something).

standard: Chị gái lái ô tô Mazda trắng va xe ô tô Ford xám đậu bên đường rồi ngã ngửa nằm ăn vạ giữa đường.
A: The woman driving the white Mazda crashed into a gray Ford parked on the side of the road, then fell over and lay down in the middle of the road pretending to be injured.
B: A woman driving a white Mazda car collided with a grey Ford car parked on the side of the road and then fell backward, lying in the middle of the road to feign injury.
C: A woman driving a white Mazda collided with a gray Ford car parked on the side of the road, then fell backward and lay down faking an injury in the middle of the road.

standard: Có cái gì dễ dễ không?
A: Is there anything easy?
B: Is there anything easy?
C: Is there anything easy?

standard: Để hôm nào đi thẩm, nhìn ngọt đã mê rồi.
A: I'll go get it checked out sometime; it looks so sweet, I'm already smitten.
B: Let's go check it out some other day; just looking at how sweet it is, I'm already hooked.
C: Some day I'll go check it out, seeing how cute it is, I'm already obsessed.

standard: tính ra vợ xem bản đồ ổn mà tại chồng lái nhầm đấy.
A: Actually, the wife was fine with the map; it was the husband who drove incorrectly.
B: Come to think of it, my wife read the map fine; it's just that my husband drove the wrong way.
C: It turns out my wife reading the map was fine, it was because I took the wrong turn.

standard: Trời mưa tối om mà sao bạn chỉnh gì sáng thế.
A: It's pitch black outside, so why did you adjust the brightness so much?
B: It's raining and pitch black, so why did you edit it to be so bright?
C: It's raining and pitch dark outside, yet you're so cheerful.

standard: Đường này nhỏ quá, xe ben vào không được nên không biết.
A: This road is too narrow, dump trucks can't get in, so I don't know.
B: This road is too narrow, dump trucks can't get in, so I didn't know.
C: This road is too narrow for a dump truck to get in, so I wouldn't know.

standard: nói không phải khen chứ muốn khui bia thật đấy
A: I'm not saying this to flatter you, but I really want to open a beer.
B: Not to sound like I'm praising, but I really want to open a beer.
C: I'm not just saying that to flatter you, but I really want to crack open a beer.

standard: cũng muốn về quê lắm rồi
A: I really want to go back to my hometown too.
B: I really want to go back to my hometown already.
C: I really want to go back home already

standard: Tau ngủ quên trời đất có nghe tiếng gì đâu .
A: I fell asleep and didn't hear a sound.
B: I overslept and didn't hear a thing.
C: I slept so soundly that I didn't hear anything at all.

standard: Hắn mà cầm dao đuổi thì cũng không sợ nhỉ.
A: If he chased me with a knife, I wouldn't be afraid, would I?
B: Even if he chased me with a knife, I wouldn't be afraid, right?
C: Even if he came at me with a knife, I wouldn't be scared though.

standard: Hồi đó vui thật, ước gì quay lại thời thơ ấu.
A: Those were such fun times. I wish I could go back to my childhood.
B: It was so much fun back then, I wish I could go back to my childhood.
C: Those times were so much fun, I wish I could go back to childhood.

standard: Nỗi niềm gì vậy Huế ơi. Mà mưa trắng trắng trời như này? (Phỏng theo thơ một người).
A: What sorrow do you feel, Hue? Why is the sky so white with rain? (Adapted from a poem by someone).
B: What are your sorrows, Hue? And why is the rain falling so heavily that it turns the whole sky white? (Adapted from someone's poetry).
C: What sorrow is this, dear Huế. And the sky so white with rain like this? (Inspired by someone's poetry)

standard: năm trước họ không cho mình lên thế này nhỉ?
A: They didn't let me go up like this last year, did they?
B: Last year they didn't let me come up here, did they?
C: Last year they didn't let me do this, huh?

standard: Chị đi về đường nào vậy?
A: Which way are you going?
B: Which way are you going home?
C: Which way are you going home?

standard: chỗ chợ lớn cháy 5 phút đen thui luôn nhỉ
A: The area around the big market was completely blackened by the fire in just 5 minutes, wasn't it?
B: The fire at Cho Lon turned everything pitch black in just 5 minutes, didn't it?
C: Just 5 minutes and the big market was already completely burned black, huh

standard: Nước này không biết Nguyễn Lít có uống không
A: I don't know if Nguyen Lit drank this water.
B: I don't know if Nguyen Lit drinks this water.
C: I wonder if Nguyễn Lít drinks this

standard: Xã nào của Lộc Hà bị thế bạn?
A: Which commune in Loc Ha district is affected like this?
B: Which commune in Loc Ha is affected, my friend?
C: Which commune in Lộc Hà is affected?

standard: được quá nhỉ.
A: That's great!
B: That's quite good, isn't it?
C: That's great!

standard: Hơi ấm ngày xưa vẫn còn đó, nhớ nhỉ?
A: The warmth of the past still lingers, doesn't it?
B: The warmth of the old days is still there, remember?
C: The warmth from the old days is still there, remember?

standard: như tôi đấy nhé, không có bia không chịu đâu.
A: Just like me, I can't stand being without beer.
B: Just like me, I can't stand it without beer.
C: Just like me, I won't put up with it without beer, you know.

standard: Bọn này không biết sợ là gì nhỉ?
A: These guys don't know what fear is, do they?
B: These people don't know what fear is, do they?
C: These people don't seem to know what fear is, huh?

standard: được người yêu đẹp mà giỏi thế này thì còn gì bằng bạn ơi
A: Having such a beautiful and talented girlfriend is the best thing ever, my friend!
B: Having a partner who is both beautiful and talented like this, it doesn't get any better than that, my friend.
C: Having a beautiful and talented girlfriend like this, there's nothing better than that, my friend!

standard: Ăn gì mà giỏi thế nhỉ?
A: What do you eat to be so smart?
B: What did you eat to be so talented?
C: What do you eat to be so good at this?

standard: Ngứa người, thèm đi rồi mà giờ đang bận chưa đi được.
A: I'm itching to go out, but I'm too busy right now.
B: I'm itching to go, but I'm busy right now and can't.
C: I'm feeling restless and really want to go already, but I'm busy right now so I can't.

standard: Mưa lụt thế này chợ không bán lấy đâu ra dưa nhỉ?
A: With all this flooding, the market can't sell anything, so where would the watermelons come from?
B: With flooding like this, the market isn't open, so where can I find any melons?
C: With flooding like this, if the market doesn't sell them, where would I even find cucumbers?

standard: Sợ không ai biết mình có tập thể dục thể thao chị ạ.
A: I'm afraid no one will know I exercise, sister.
B: I'm afraid no one will know that I exercise, sister.
C: I'm afraid that no one knows I exercise, sis.

standard: thương quá mẹ ạ, không khổ nào bằng.
A: Oh, Mom, I feel so sorry for you. There's no suffering like this.
B: I feel so much pity, Mom; there is no suffering worse than this.
C: I love you so much, Mom. No suffering compares to this.

standard: Rồi từ nay vắng bóng người qua ngồi em nhỉ.
A: From now on, no one will come to sit here anymore, will they?
B: And from now on, there will be no one sitting here, right?
C: And so from now on, no one will come by and sit with me anymore, huh.

standard: Đường nhà có chút xíu, 3 trạm.
A: The road home is very short, with only 3 stops.
B: The road to my house is very short, only 3 stops.
C: The route from my house is just a little bit, 3 stops.

standard: về quê đúc bánh xèo ăn chị hai ơi
A: Let's go back to our hometown and make Vietnamese savory pancakes, sister!
B: Going back to my hometown to make bánh xèo to eat, big sister!
C: Going back to the countryside to make bánh xèo and eat, older sister!

standard: người bữa nay đông thật bà nhỉ?
A: There are so many people today, aren't there, Grandma?
B: There are really a lot of people today, aren't there?
C: It's really crowded today, isn't it?

standard: bỏ rơi cái gì mà bỏ rơi, khó hiểu thế nhỉ.
A: Abandon what? That's so confusing.
B: What do you mean by "abandon," it's so confusing.
C: What's being abandoned? I don't get it, huh.

standard: lập hội đi được đấy nhỉ.
A: Forming a club would be a good idea, wouldn't it?
B: Starting a group for this would be a good idea, wouldn't it?
C: We should form a group, that could work, huh.

standard: được mùa cua hay sao mà mâm nào cũng canh cua vậy?
A: Is it a crab season or something? Every table is covered in crab soup!
B: Is it a bumper crop of crabs or something that every single tray has crab soup?
C: Is it crab season or something? There's crab soup at every single meal like this.

standard: Khen thế này là năm nào bác cũng cho ăn bánh canh hết đó. Dì khen món nào ngon là bác cho dì ăn món đó tận 1 tháng cơ
A: With such praise, Uncle will treat you to banh canh (Vietnamese noodle soup) every year. If Auntie praises a dish as delicious, Uncle will let her eat it for a whole month!
B: If you praise it like this, he'll give you banh canh every year. Whenever you praise a dish as being delicious, he'll make you eat that dish for an entire month.
C: Praising like this, is there any year when you won't let us eat bánh canh every day. Whenever my aunt praises a dish as delicious, you let her eat that same dish for a whole month

standard: Cá heo miền Tây ngon tuyệt vời.
A: Western dolphin fish is absolutely delicious.
B: Western region dolphins are wonderful.
C: Western region dolphin is extremely delicious.

standard: Ở gần mới chạy lui chạy tới coi con ra sao. Chứ xa làm sao mà chạy
A: If you live nearby, you can run back and forth to check on your child. But if you live far away, how can you run?
B: I'm only staying nearby so I can go back and forth to see how the baby is doing. If I were far away, there's no way I could rush back.
C: Only when it's nearby can I run back and forth to check on it. But if it's far, how can I even run there?

standard: Mãi đẳng cấp chị ơi. Không xạo không nịnh. Nể nhất chị là người sống thẳng.
A: You're always so classy, ​​sister. No pretense, no flattery. What I admire most about you is that you're a straightforward person.
B: Always top-tier, sister. No lying, no flattery. What I admire most about you is that you live honestly.
C: Always so classy, sis. No BS, no sucking up. What I respect most about you is that you're a straightforward person.

standard: là ở đâu nhỉ?
A: Where is that?
B: Where is it?
C: I wonder where it is.

standard: Ở cầu Phủ chứ ở đâu nữa
A: At Phu Bridge, where else?
B: At Phu Bridge, where else?
C: Where else would it be but at Cầu Phủ?

standard: Ra đầu ngõ mua 5 nghìn dưa muối với mấy quả trứng nữa.
A: I went to the end of the street to buy 5,000 dong worth of pickled cucumbers and a few eggs.
B: Go to the end of the alley to buy 5 thousand dong worth of pickled watermelon and a few eggs.
C: I went to the corner of the street and bought 5,000 dong worth of pickled vegetables along with a few eggs too.

standard: Hôm nay hoạt động chưa hay ngày mai vậy chị?
A: Are you open today or tomorrow?
B: Are you operating today or tomorrow?
C: Is it active today or tomorrow, sister?

standard: Chứ tao đâu nói mày đầu trâu đâu
A: I didn't say you had a bull's head.
B: But I didn't call you a blockhead.
C: But I didn't call you a buffalo head

standard: Nhớ anh nào ở Huế rồi đúng không? Hôm qua đến giờ thấy mày hơi nhiều đấy.
A: Remember that guy from Hue? I've seen you around quite a bit since yesterday.
B: You miss some guy in Hue, don't you? From yesterday until now, I've seen you [doing/posting] quite a lot.
C: Remember that guy in Hue, right? Since yesterday, I've noticed you've been around quite a bit.

standard: Vậy thì gần. Tưởng chỗ nào xa chứ anh ở quận 12 sát bên. Cuối tuần em rảnh không? Ta đi chơi
A: That's close. I thought it was somewhere far away, but I live right next door in District 12. Are you free this weekend? Let's hang out.
B: That's close then. I thought it would be somewhere far, but I'm in District 12 right next door. Are you free this weekend? Let's go out.
C: So it's close then. I thought it was somewhere far, but I live in District 12 right next to it. Are you free this weekend? Let's go hang out

standard: Mùa thu đẹp thôi rồi.
A: Autumn is absolutely beautiful.
B: Autumn is incredibly beautiful.
C: Autumn is beautiful, but it's almost over.

standard: Làm như béo lắm nữa mà sợ.
A: It's as if you're so fat that you're afraid.
B: Acting like I'm so fat, yet I'm still afraid.

standard: Món gì lạ vậy nhỉ?

standard: Mấy mẹ này rảnh ngứa miệng, phạt 50 triệu đi cho khiếp
A: These women are just idle and gossipy; fine them 50 million VND to teach them a lesson.
B: These women have too much free time and a big mouth; fine them 50 million to scare them.
C: These women have nothing better to do than run their mouths—fine them 50 million to scare them.

standard: Đợt này sao toàn chị em nhảy số nhanh vậy ta?
A: Why are all the women jumping to conclusions so quickly this time?
B: Why are all the ladies so quick to catch on this time?
C: Why are all of us ladies getting numbers so fast these days?

standard: nghe có gì sai sai, đại gia quận Cam gì lạ vậy
A: Something sounds off here, what kind of strange Orange County tycoon is he?
B: Something sounds off, what kind of strange "Orange District tycoon" is this?
C: Something sounds a bit off, what's so weird about this rich person from Orange County

standard: Chỉnh màu lên phát nhìn khác hẳn, ngon ghê
A: Adjusting the colors makes a huge difference, it looks amazing!
B: Adjusting the colors makes it look completely different, looks great.
C: Just adjusting the colors a bit makes it look completely different, it's really good

A: If you're not going in, just leave. Why bother stopping by for a bit?
B: If you weren't going to come in, you should have just kept going; I don't know why you bothered stopping by for a bit.
C: If you're not going to come in, then just go ahead and leave. What was the point of stopping by for a bit?

standard: Gọi một ly trà tắc bà ấy tặng kèm theo một cặp vú sữa tráng miệng.
A: I ordered a glass of lime tea and she gave me a pair of custard apples as a dessert.
B: I ordered a glass of kumquat tea, and she gave me a pair of star apples for dessert.
C: She ordered a glass of tamarind tea and was given a complimentary pair of star apples for dessert.

standard: Đau óc với bọn ngu này.
A: These idiots are driving me crazy.
B: My head hurts from dealing with these idiots.
C: This is giving me a headache with these idiots.

standard: Về tới xóm Chùa sao tao không nhớ gì hết.
A: When I got back to Chùa village, I couldn't remember anything.
B: When I got back to Chua hamlet, why don't I remember anything?
C: I arrived at Chùa village and don't remember anything at all.

standard: Thấy giống giống chứ không biết chỗ nào.
A: It looks similar, but I can't pinpoint exactly where.
B: It looks similar, but I don't know where.
C: It looks similar, but I don't know where.

standard: Ngon quá, có ai mua không nhỉ?
A: It looks delicious, would anyone like to buy some?
B: So delicious, I wonder if anyone is buying it?
C: So delicious, is anyone interested in buying it?

standard: hôm bữa em mới chở cái anh kia đi trên đấy này.
A: I gave that guy a ride up there the other day.
B: The other day, I just gave that guy a ride on there.
C: The other day I just drove that guy up there.

standard: Tiêu rồi bà con ạ.
A: It's over, folks.
B: I'm doomed, everyone.
C: It's over, everyone.

standard: đang chụp hình hay sao mà không thấy nhỉ?
A: Are they taking pictures or something? I can't see them.
B: Are you taking photos or something? Why don't I see you?
C: Are we taking photos or what? Why can't I see anything?

standard: Bạn tôi nó đi bên đấy giờ đi trỏng luôn rồi.
A: My friend went over there and is now gone for good.
B: My friend went over there and is now staying there for good.
C: My friend went over there and has now already gone straight there.

standard: Tại sao đi đám cưới bà tập yoga? Nói nghe xem
A: Why did you do yoga at the wedding? Tell me.
B: Why do you do yoga at a wedding? Tell me about it.
C: Why did you go to a wedding and practice yoga? Tell me about it.

standard: đợi chờ từ hè tới giờ.
A: I've been waiting since summer.
B: I've been waiting from the summer until now.
C: I've been waiting from summer until now.

standard: Thầy phán em 4 đứa gái. Để em báo vợ đẻ tiếp cho chị nhận nhé, chứ mấy đứa này lớn quá rồi.
A: The fortune teller predicted I'd have four daughters. I'll tell my wife to have more so you can adopt them, because these ones are getting too big already.
B: The teacher predicted I'd have four girls. Let me tell my wife to have more so you can adopt them, because these ones are already too old.
C: The fortune teller predicted I'd have 4 girls. Let me tell my wife to keep having babies so you can take them, because these ones are already too big.

standard: Sim chứ chery đâu ra.
A: It's a sim card, not a cherry.
B: It's a SIM, where did "cherry" come from?
C: It's a SIM, but where did cherry come from?

standard: Khi nào cho được như thế nhỉ?

B: When will I be able to be like that?
C: When will I be able to get to that point?

standard: Thành phố sao mà nhanh lụt thế.
A: Why does the city flood so quickly?
B: Why does the city flood so quickly?
C: How come the city floods so fast?

standard: Ê chúng mày, đi đi chúng mày, biển ở trên đấy đẹp lắm.
A: Hey guys, let's go, the beach up there is beautiful.
B: Hey guys, go there, the beach up there is beautiful.
C: Hey guys, let's go, you guys, the beach up there is really beautiful.

A: I only admit that I'm a bit mean with my mouth, and nobody can argue with that.
B: I only admit that I'm a bit sly and that no one can outtalk me.
C: I only admit I'm a bit cheeky with my mouth, just because no one can talk back, that's all.

standard: Mẹ con gặt bằng liềm như thật. Cái đầu bọn này không biết để đâu nhỉ?
A: The mother and daughter are harvesting with sickles as if they were real. Where do these kids even put their heads?
B: My mother is harvesting with a sickle just like the real thing. I wonder where these people have their heads?
C: Mother and child harvesting with a sickle, looking so real. I wonder where on earth they'll put the heads on these?

standard: Đô thị gì mà đường xá tối thui
A: What kind of city is this where the streets are pitch black?
B: What kind of urban area has such pitch-black roads?
C: What kind of city is this where the streets are pitch dark

standard: Thảo nào xúm lại đông khủng khiếp
A: No wonder there was such a huge crowd.
B: No wonder there's such a huge crowd gathering.
C: No wonder they're crowded together like that

standard: Nại Cửu sát nhập vô Đông Hà khi nào thế nhỉ?
A: When did Nai Cuu merge with Dong Ha?
B: When was Nai Cuu merged into Dong Ha?
C: When was Nại Cửu annexed into Đông Hà, I wonder?

A: So how are you doing now? I heard about your situation, but I don't know what to wish you.
B: So how are you now? I'm hearing this, but I really don't know what to wish you.
C: So how are you now? I heard about it, but I don't even know what to wish for you.

standard: Tướng mày mà đậu xách xe ra đường đi ngớp ngớp vậy đền họ không nổi
A: If you, General, park your car and drive around like that, you won't be able to afford to compensate them.
B: With a forehead like that, if you take the bike out on the road and cause an accident, you won't be able to afford the compensation.
C: Look at you, if you park and drive that badly on the road, you won't even be able to compensate them

standard: Ngon tuyệt vời!
A: Absolutely delicious!
B: Absolutely delicious!
C: Amazingly delicious!

standard: Chỉnh - bóp - chỉnh mịn da, đủ kiểu mới được như thế này đó.
A: Adjusting, smoothing, and refining the skin—all sorts of techniques are needed to achieve this look.
B: Adjusting, slimming, smoothing the skin—all sorts of things had to be done to make it look like this.
C: Edited - reshaped - smoothed skin, all kinds of techniques just to get it like this.

B: Is anyone going back to Cho Moi, An Giang?
C: Is anyone going back to Chợ Mới, An Giang?

B: Stop by the spicy noodle shop I eat at; I also have a place open nearby.

A: Who sells such delicious food?
B: Who is selling such good stuff?
C: Who's selling such good stuff?

A: Let's go buy some ingredients this afternoon so we can start our business, okay?
B: Which afternoon should I go buy it and then get to work?
C: Which afternoon should we go buy (something) and eat together?

A: It's been raining too much lately, hasn't it? If the sun comes out nicely, we'll go tomorrow, okay?
B: It's been raining a lot lately, hasn't it? If the weather is sunny and beautiful, we'll go tomorrow, right?
C: It's been raining a lot lately, right? If the weather is nice and sunny, let's go tomorrow, shall we?

standard: Vậy tấm chiếu cũ thì như thế nào?

B: So what about the old mat?
C: So how about the old mat?

A: We play every year, but this year I'm starting to worry about my grandparents' loose change, guys.

C: Why do I keep seeing you on every group?

A: This is my favorite part; I still feel happy remembering it.
B: I love this part the most; remembering it still makes me happy.
C: I love this thing the most, I still find it fun when I recall it

standard: Xem làm sao thì biết thôi.
A: We'll find out when we see how it happens.
B: We'll see and then we'll know.
C: Just see how it's done and you'll know.

B: I'm afraid of drowning when swimming, sister.
C: I want to go swimming but I'm scared of drowning, sis

C: Why are you being so blunt like that, anh?

standard: úi trời cháy ghê quá em!
A: Oh my god, the fire is terrible!

B: Just a country person coming to the city to study.
C: Just a villager heading to the city to study.

B: I released the fish there, who caught them?

A: I only get one or two dates a year, and each one lasts almost half a day.

A: So, do you know where the Friendship Village Gate is?
B: So do you know where the Friendship Village gate is?
C: So do you know where the Friendship Village gate is?

A: Will there be a basketball court later?
B: Will there be a basketball court in the future?
C: Will there be a basketball court in the future?

standard: Thấy ở đâu bị tốc mái nhà đấy, hết hồn.
A: Everywhere I saw houses with roofs blown off, it was terrifying.
B: I saw a roof being blown off somewhere over there, it gave me a fright.
C: Saw somewhere with the roof blown off, I was so scared.

A: You have to roll up your pants and hang your shoes around your neck to wade through the mud.
B: I also have to roll up my pants and hang my shoes around my neck to wade through the mud.

B: Where in Ha Tinh is this person from? Congratulations on your success.

B: Want some electroshock, beautiful?
C: What, you won't get shocked, beautiful?

A: This Nghe An page keeps posting, all they do is show off this house, but I haven't seen them donate a single penny.
B: This Nghe An page also posts constantly; I only see them showing off this house, but I haven't seen them donate a single cent.
C: This Nghệ An page also keeps posting all the time, always just shows off this house but I've never seen them support anything.

standard: Giá như bây giờ như vậy thì tao ra ôm mày một cái rồi về đấy
A: If things were like this now, I'd come give you a hug and then go home.
B: If it were like that right now, I would go out and give you a hug and then go home.
C: If things were like this now, I'd go hug you and then head back.

A: I bought a bag of apples a while ago but didn't eat a single one. But when friends came over, I sat down and ate them like I was addicted to apples.

C: Bought a bag of apples but haven't eaten a single one lately, it's just whenever friends come over they sit and eat them like apple addicts

A: Buy now and save up; you'll have more next year.
B: Buy a size up to allow for growth next year.

A: Even though I'm not a student, I know this.
B: I'm not a student, but even I know this.

A: There's nothing beautiful about it. When it rains in Hue, you'll starve to death, so there's nothing beautiful about it.

C: It's not beautiful at all. When it rains in Hue, you literally starve to death, so what's beautiful about that.

A: This intersection is very wide, and there are no traffic lights yet, ma'am.
B: This intersection is very wide, but there are no traffic lights yet, ma'am.
C: This four-way intersection here is really wide, but there aren't any traffic lights yet, auntie.

standard: Làm mau lên, tao vào Sài Gòn ở với
A: Hurry up, I'm going to Saigon to live with you.
B: Hurry up, I'm going to Saigon to stay with you.
C: Hurry up, I'm going to stay in Saigon

standard: Giao bánh cho vợ ra tới Quảng Bình luôn. Không biết xăng xe nào cho nổi
A: I'm delivering the cakes to my wife all the way to Quang Binh. I don't know how I'll manage to afford the gas for the whole trip.
B: Delivering cakes to my wife all the way in Quang Binh. I don't know how I can afford the gas.
C: Delivering cakes to my wife all the way to Quảng Bình. Wondering if this gas will even be enough for the trip.

B: No other travel destination compares to this place.
C: Nowhere else compares to traveling here.

standard: Xã này lạ nhỉ?

B: This commune is strange, isn't it?
C: This commune is weird, huh?

standard: Ối trời ơi! Chín ròn tới đâu bóc ăn tới đó, đến lúc chín đem ra còn bộ xương.
A: Oh my goodness! They're so crispy that you have to peel and eat them as soon as they're cooked, and by the time you take them out, all that's left is the bones.

C: Oh my god! It was so ripe that wherever it was peeled, they ate it right there, and by the time it was fully ripe and brought out, there was only the skeleton left.

standard: Tao đoán nhiều người sẽ nghĩ trong đầu là mua cái này, rồi ăn cái kia, đi chỗ này rồi đi chỗ khác.

B: I guess many people will be thinking to themselves about buying this, then eating that, going to this place and then that other place.
C: I bet many people will think in their heads about buying this, then eating that, going here and then going somewhere else.

standard: Tiền đâu ra mà lắm thế?
A: Where did all that money come from?
B: Where did all that money come from?
C: Where does all that money come from?

standard: Làm gì làm, tao phun bình thuốc đã.

B: Whatever you're doing, I'm going to spray the pesticide first.

B: Chives with a wonderful savory fermented fish dipping sauce.
C: Chives with mắm kho dipping sauce, incredibly delicious.

B: I've eaten it before. But it was prepared in ball form, not eaten whole like this.

A: It's so cold, and I don't even have money to go out to eat.

C: It's this cold and I don't have money to go out to eat, huh.

A: So, give the youngest one a few boxes.
B: Then give me a few cases.
C: Then give me a few boxes.

standard: Có thấy gì đâu.
A: I don't see anything.
B: I don't see anything.
C: There's nothing to see here at all.

standard: Mới đầu 3 chứ mấy mà già nhỉ?
A: I'm only 30, how can I be old then?
B: Just barely in the 30s, but why so old?
C: Just 3 years old at first, not even that many, yet already considered old?

standard: Bạn hỏi coi suất nào miễn phí bên Đức này không. Chứ tôi cũng khó khăn.
A: You should ask if there are any free places available here in Germany. I'm also facing financial difficulties.
B: Ask if there are any free slots here in Germany. Because I am also struggling.
C: Can you check if there are any free spots in Germany? I'm also struggling.

standard: Tự té vô chứ có ai biết gì đâu anh hai.
A: He fell in by himself; nobody knew anything about it, brother.
B: I just fell on my own, no one knows anything about it, big brother.
C: I just fell on my own, what would anyone know anyway, big brother.

standard: Bộ đội bữa nay sướng mà mấy ông ở làng nói vậy
A: The soldiers are having a good time these days, that's what the guys in the village are saying.
B: Soldiers these days have it easy, or so the men in the village say.
C: The troops feel so good today, but you guys in the village say that.

standard: Quảng Ninh giàu tình nghĩa, giàu tiền bạc.
A: Quang Ninh is rich in kindness and rich in money.
B: Quang Ninh is rich in both affection and money.
C: Quảng Ninh is rich in heartfelt bonds and rich in wealth.

standard: Lúc nhỏ đi giữ bò. Đốt quả pháo này sao nó không nổ nhỉ?
A: When I was a kid, I used to herd cows. I lit this firecracker, but why didn't it explode?
B: When I was little, I went to herd cows. Why didn't this firecracker explode when I lit it?
C: When I was young, I went to herd cattle. I lit this firecracker, why doesn't it explode?

standard: Ai lại đi đồn. Có thật mà đồn làm gì mất công vậy?
A: Why would anyone spread rumors? If it's true, why bother spreading them?
B: Who is spreading rumors? It's true, so why go through the trouble of spreading rumors?
C: Who would go around spreading rumors like that? If it's true, why bother talking about it? What a waste of effort.

standard: Lên rừng chơi không mày?
A: Want to go hiking in the forest?
B: Want to go to the forest for fun?
C: Do you want to go to the forest and hang out?

standard: Nhà tôi nay gia nhập Hà Tĩnh với nha.
A: My family has now joined Ha Tinh province.
B: My family is joining Ha Tinh today.
C: My family has now moved to Hà Tĩnh with me, okay?

standard: Đi trên đường gió nó thổi, tí nữa thì đi luôn.
A: The wind was blowing so hard while we were walking that we almost passed out.
B: The wind was blowing while I was on the road, and I almost blew away.
C: Walking on the road, the wind was blowing so hard I almost got blown away.

standard: bạn Khang nay có hay hỏi như thế không?
A: Does Khang often ask questions like that these days?
B: Does Khang often ask things like that today?
C: Has Khang been asking like that lately?

standard: Nhìn là biết không qua rồi. Không gầy cỡ đó đâu.
A: It's obvious he didn't pass. He's not that skinny.
B: One look and I can tell it's not them. They aren't that thin.
C: Just by looking, I can tell it won't pass. It's not that thin at all.

standard: Cá gì chứ có phải cá trồi đâu.
A: It's not some kind of fish, but a type of fish that comes up into the water.
B: What kind of fish is it? It's certainly not a climbing perch.
C: What kind of fish is this, it's definitely not snakehead fish.

standard: Cái gì mà tag tôi hoài vậy mà.
A: Why do you keep tagging me?
B: Why do you keep tagging me?
C: Why do you keep tagging me like that.

standard: Cái gì cũng biết nhỉ. Nhà tao sát bên mà tôi cũng không biết gì.
A: You seem to know everything. My house is right next door, and I don't know anything either.
B: You know everything, don't you? My house is right next door, and even I don't know anything.
C: You really do know everything, don't you? My house is right next door yet I don't know anything about it either.

standard: Không biết khi nào mới có cầu Gianh. Chứ cứ đi qua là rung rung.
A: I don't know when the Gianh Bridge will be built. Every time I cross it, it shakes.
B: I don't know when the Gianh bridge will finally be ready. Because every time I cross it, it shakes.
C: I don't know when we'll have the Gianh bridge. Every time I cross it, it shakes.

standard: Cái bánh này mà ném vào mặt nyc chắc ở Hà Nội cũng nghe thấy nhỉ?
A: If this cake were thrown in my ex's face, I bet people in Hanoi would hear about it, right?
B: If I threw this cake at my ex's face, people in Hanoi would probably hear it, wouldn't they?
C: This cake, if I threw it at my ex's face, people in Hanoi would probably hear it too, huh?

standard: Đọc cái đó xong làm tao tưởng mày.
A: After reading that, it made me think it was you.
B: After reading that, I thought it was you.
C: After reading that, I thought you were...

standard: không có công an thì mình không biết ông giàu hơn mình, trời ơi!
A: If the police weren't around, I wouldn't have known he was richer than me, oh my God!
B: If it weren't for the police, I wouldn't have known he was richer than me, oh my god!
C: Without the police, I wouldn't know that guy's richer than me, oh my god!

standard: Mà bên này ông đấy lắp ngay máy giặt cho tao rồi, tao không được đổi nữa đâu.
A: But the guy over here already installed the washing machine for me, so I can't change it anymore.
B: But on this side, that guy already installed the washing machine for me, so I can't exchange it anymore.
C: But he already installed the washing machine for me here, and I can't change it anymore.

standard: Thế này thì đi Sài Gòn nhanh như đi chợ phố em nhỉ?
A: With this, getting to Saigon will be as quick as going to the street market, right?
B: With this, going to Saigon is as quick as going to the local market, isn't it?
C: Wow, getting to Saigon is now as quick as going to the street market for me, huh?

standard: Lâu rồi chưa ăn bánh ướt ram Hà Tĩnh.
A: It's been a long time since I last ate Ha Tinh-style steamed rice rolls with fried spring rolls.
B: It's been a long time since I've had Ha Tinh steamed rice rolls with fried shrimp cakes.
C: It's been a long time since I've had bánh ướt ram from Hà Tĩnh.

standard: Ở nhà trùm chăn cho lành.
A: Stay home and wrap yourself in a blanket, it's safer.
B: It's safer to stay at home and curl up under the blanket.
C: Staying home wrapped in a blanket is good for you.

standard: loại cờ bạc, ông tổ của thằng bần, cứ thích lấy nhà họ làm chuồng heo nhà mình
A: Gambling, the root of poverty, always wants to use other people's houses as his own pigsty.
B: Gambling, the ancestor of poverty, just loves to turn their houses into my own pigsties.
C: that gambling scumbag, the progenitor of that poor wretch, always likes to take their house and make it a pigsty for his own home

standard: xe đạp vượt ẩu chứ không phải xe ben đâu
A: It was a cyclist who was recklessly overtaking, not a dump truck.
B: It was a bicycle overtaking recklessly, not a dump truck.
C: A bicycle was overtaking recklessly, not a dump truck at all.

standard: Giữ lưng quần lại chứ không cũng bị lấy.
A: Hold onto your pants or they'll get stolen.
B: Hold onto your waistband, otherwise it might get taken too.
C: Hold onto the waistband, otherwise it'll get taken too.

standard: Vậy khi nào có kèo vậy mấy bạn?
A: So when will the match be available, guys?
B: So when is the plan happening, guys?
C: So when is there a match, you guys?

standard: Làm gì? Bão thì trường đâu có dạy.
A: What to do? The school doesn't teach about storms.
B: What should I do? Schools don't teach you about storms.
C: Doing what? Schools don't teach about storms.

standard: Bảo hiểm lần này lãnh đủ bạn nhỉ?
A: This time, the insurance company paid the full amount, didn't they?
B: The insurance payout this time is in full, right?
C: Got the full insurance payout this time, right?

standard: Vậy xin không khí trong này làm gì, tưởng ra đó là hạnh phúc rồi chứ.
A: So why ask for fresh air in here? I thought happiness would be out there.
B: Then why seek the atmosphere in here? I thought going out there was happiness.
C: So what's the point of longing for the air here, I thought happiness was out there already.

standard: Bà ngoại mới thấy chưa? Có khi bà bắt xây 1 cái nữa!
A: Grandma just saw it, right? Maybe she'll make us build another one!
B: Did Grandma see it yet? She might make us build another one!
C: Have you just seen it? Grandma might just make us build another one!

standard: 2 ly mà cỡ đó rồi đó, 5, 6 ly thì sao nữa trời
A: That's already that much for 2 glasses, imagine what it would be like with 5 or 6 glasses!
B: Two glasses are already that size, so what will happen with 5 or 6 glasses?
C: Already that big at just 2 glasses, what about 5 or 6, goodness

standard: Chị này làm gì mà giàu vậy.
A: How did this woman get so rich?
B: What does this woman do to be so rich?
C: What does she do to be so rich?

standard: Gái Hà Tĩnh đẹp thế chúng mày.
A: The girls from Ha Tinh are so beautiful, guys.
B: Hà Tĩnh girls are so beautiful, you guys.
C: Ha Tinh girls are so beautiful, you guys.

standard: Chắc lên làm một kèo chứ nhỉ?
A: Should we make a bet?
B: Maybe we should set up a plan?
C: Should probably do a project, hmm?

standard: Đi gần bên em đi. Do Gò Vấp nó nhiều chỗ đi hơn.
A: Go somewhere closer to me. Because Go Vap has more places to go.
B: Walk near me. Because Go Vap has more places to go.
C: Go near me. Gò Vấp has more places to go anyway.

standard: Bố vợ xây nhà to nhất huyện như này, làm gì dại đến mức xây trên đất nông nghiệp.
A: My father-in-law built the biggest house in the district like this; he wouldn't be so foolish as to build it on agricultural land.
B: My father-in-law built the biggest house in the district like this; why would he be foolish enough to build it on agricultural land?
C: My father-in-law is building the biggest house in the district like this—there's no way he'd be foolish enough to build it on agricultural land.

standard: 2 chị đến đâu là bờ trắng đến đó
A: Wherever the two sisters went, white sand followed.
B: Wherever the two sisters go, the white shore follows.
C: Wherever the 2 sisters go, white sand follows there

standard: Tiêu luôn, thằng này kiếp này coi như bỏ.
A: He's doomed, this guy's life is over.
B: It's over, this guy is a lost cause in this lifetime.
C: It's over. This guy, I might as well just give up on in this lifetime.

standard: Ngày nào đó đi chữa lành nhỉ?
A: Maybe we should go for healing sometime?
B: Shall we go heal ourselves some day?
C: Someday I should go somewhere healing, hmm?

standard: Bà có về không mà bà hay bàn quá vây.
A: Is she even coming home? She talks so much about it.
B: Are you even coming back, or why are you talking so much?
C: Are you going home or not, that you're always commenting too much?

standard: Trúng cái thì biết đâu có vợ.
A: If you're lucky, you might even find a wife.
B: If I win one, who knows, I might get a wife.
C: If I win, maybe I'd get a wife.

standard: Đâu ra mà nhìn khiếp vậy nhỉ?
A: Where did that terrifying thing come from?
B: Where did that come from that it looks so terrifying?
C: Where did that come from? It looks so scary!

standard: Mày thì cái gì cũng hay cả.
A: You're good at everything.
B: You think you're always right about everything.
C: You're good at everything.

standard: làm tôi phải gọi điện mẹ xem thật không.
A: It made me call my mom to see if it was true.
B: It made me have to call my mother to see if it was true.
C: This makes me have to call mom to check if it's true.

standard: Ăn xong, mày để mẹ rửa bát rồi đi chơi luôn.
A: After you finish eating, let Mom wash the dishes and then go play.
B: After eating, let mom wash the dishes and then go out immediately.
C: After eating, you left mom to wash the dishes and went out to play right away.

standard: Trả chứ tao không lấy, con rể tặng thì lấy chứ của chúng mày lấy làm gì
A: I'll give it back, I'm not taking it. If my son-in-law gave it to me, I'll take it, but what would I do with what you guys would take?
B: I'll return it because I'm not taking it; if my son-in-law gave it, I'd take it, but why would I take it from you guys?
C: Give it back then, I won't take it. If the son-in-law gives it, I'll take it. Why would I take yours?

standard: Cầu này ở chỗ nào Quảng Bình vậy huynh?
A: Where in Quang Binh province is this bridge located, brother?
B: Where in Quang Binh is this bridge, brother?
C: Where is this bridge in Quảng Bình, brother?

standard: Chị cảm ơn. Trong này không ảnh hưởng gì cả em ạ. Ngoài đó sao rồi, gia đình ổn cả chứ.
A: Thank you. Nothing is affecting things here, dear. How are things out there? Is your family alright?
B: Thank you. It doesn't affect anything in here. How are things out there, is your family doing well?
C: Thank you, sis. It doesn't affect me at all here. How are things over there, is the family doing well?

standard: Không cho đá sân chợ Trụ đâu nhỉ?
A: They won't let us play football at Tru Market, will they?
B: They won't let us play football at the Tru market pitch, right?
C: I'm not allowed to play at Chợ Trụ field, I wonder why?

standard: Nó về tận nhà đánh cho đấy, nói ngu ngơ vậy anh
A: He'll come all the way home and beat you up, you idiot!
B: They came all the way to the house to beat me up, why are you talking so cluelessly?
C: It went all the way to the house to beat them up, saying something so stupid like that, anh

standard: Ở nhà không làm gì thì đi giúp đỡ bà con với em.
A: If you have nothing to do at home, go help your neighbors with me.
B: If you're at home doing nothing, come and help the people with me.
C: If you're not doing anything at home, let's go help our relatives together.

standard: Phải giống nhau thế chứ có đứa giỏi hơn cho ra khỏi hội liền.
A: They have to be the same; if someone is better, they'll be kicked out of the group immediately.
B: We have to be alike; if anyone is better, they'll be kicked out of the group immediately.
C: They all have to be the same, or if someone's better, they get kicked out of the group right away.

standard: Nghệ An chúng tôi tình cảm vậy đó, khi nào các bạn cũng nói chúng tôi keo kẹt, sống tiết kiệm.
A: That's how warm-hearted we are in Nghe An, yet you always say we're stingy and frugal.
B: We from Nghe An are that affectionate, even though you all always say we are stingy and live frugally.
C: That's how affectionate we people from Nghệ An are, yet you always say we're stingy and live too frugally.

standard: Chẳng lẽ bây giờ còn 10 cái nữa thì cất để uống à mọi người ơi
A: Should we save the remaining 10 for ourselves to drink later, everyone?
B: Is it possible that if there are still 10 more, I should just put them away to drink later, everyone?
C: Could it be that with 10 left now, we should put them away to drink, everyone?

standard: Kì dáng mi nhon mất từ khi nào không biết. Bây giờ đoạn thiếu đoạn thừa nhìn khiếp đảm
A: I don't know when she lost her slender figure. Now, she looks horrifying with her missing and excess parts.
B: I don't know when my slim figure disappeared. Now, with some parts lacking and others excessive, it looks terrible.
C: I don't know when the thin brow shape disappeared. Now with patches missing and patches overgrown, it looks terrifying

standard: Để bữa sau tôi lên mạng làm thử cái xem sao. Nghe mấy người trên mạng họ nói thấy cũng khó khiếp đó.
A: I'll try it online next time. I heard some people online saying it's incredibly difficult.
B: I'll try doing it online next time to see how it goes. Hearing people online talk about it, it sounds incredibly difficult.
C: I'll try making it online later. I heard people online say it was really difficult though.

standard: Không biết hôm nào mình đi thì còn không nhỉ?
A: I wonder if they'll still be available when I go?
B: I wonder if it will still be there by the time I go?
C: I wonder if I can still go on some day.

standard: Sóc Trăng tao gọi là cốm ống nhé chúng mày
A: In Soc Trang, I call it "com ong" (tube-shaped rice flakes), guys.
B: In Soc Trang, I call it cốm ống, you guys.
C: In Sóc Trăng, we call it cốm ống, you all

standard: Trúng xe máy thật luôn chúng mày ơi.
A: We actually won a motorbike, guys!
B: I actually won a motorcycle, you guys!
C: I really got hit by a motorcycle you guys.

standard: Tiền thiếu gì mà cực thế.
A: Why are you struggling so much when you have so much money?
B: Why is it so hard when you're lacking money.
C: Why is it so hard when money is tight?

standard: Em đi siêu thị mà dì đấy nói “già nhỉ” là dì đang khen hay chê em vậy mấy cô mấy chú ơi?
A: I went to the supermarket and the lady there said, "You look old," was she complimenting me or criticizing me, ladies and gentlemen?
B: I went to the supermarket and that auntie said "so old," so is she praising or criticizing me, everyone?
C: I went to the supermarket and that auntie said "you're old, huh" — is she complimenting me or criticizing me, uncles and aunts?

standard: bia ngon mà cái bao bì cũng đẹp nữa chứ.
A: The beer is delicious, and the packaging is beautiful too.
B: The beer is delicious, and the packaging is beautiful too.
C: The beer is delicious and the packaging is nice too.

standard: Ủa vậy là sao ta?
A: Huh, what does that mean?
B: Wait, how does that work?
C: Wait, so what do you mean?

standard: Không mưa đâu, nắng bể đầu.
A: It's not raining, it's scorching hot.
B: It's not raining; it's scorching hot.
C: No rain, just scorching sun.

standard: Ở Triệu Phong cũng giống vậy
A: It's the same in Trieu Phong.
B: It's the same in Trieu Phong.
C: It's the same in Triệu Phong.

standard: mai sinh nhật mày, đi luôn nhỉ.
A: It's your birthday tomorrow, let's go!
B: Tomorrow is your birthday, let's just go.
C: Tomorrow's your birthday, let's just go ahead now, huh?

standard: giờ rủ nó không đi đâu, phải rủ đi uống nước ngọt mới chịu đi
A: Now, if you invite her anywhere, she won't go; you have to invite her for a soft drink for her to agree to go.
B: If I invite them to go anywhere now, they won't go; I have to invite them to go drink soft drinks for them to agree to go.
C: Now inviting them doesn't work at all, I have to ask them to go get some soda first before they'll agree to come.

standard: Đứa nào cũng chăm thế này có phải là cô đỡ đau đầu rồi không?
A: If everyone were this attentive, wouldn't I have fewer headaches?
B: Since everyone is this hardworking, doesn't that mean you don't have to worry as much, teacher?
C: If each and every one of them is this diligent, does that mean you won't have to worry as much anymore?

standard: gặp mình thì chiếc wave đó qua tận Lào rồi nhỉ?
A: If I ever met him, that Wave motorbike would have already made it all the way to Laos, right?
B: If you had met me, that Wave would have gone all the way to Laos by now, wouldn't it?
C: So that Wave already went all the way to Laos?

standard: Chưa được chụp luôn ấy. Đợi bà ấy nhé
A: The photo hasn't been taken yet. Let's wait for her.
B: It hasn't even been taken yet. Wait for her.
C: It hasn't been taken yet. Wait for her.

standard: Tôi cắt cỡ này này, bao này thì ăn thua gì?
A: I cut it to this size, but what good is this bag?
B: I cut it to this size; what does this amount even matter?
C: What difference does it make if I cut it this big, this bag?

standard: Trời ơi vui tuyệt vời luôn ấy.
A: Oh my god, it was so much fun!
B: Oh my god, it's absolutely wonderful.
C: Oh my goodness, I'm so incredibly happy!

standard: Mấy ông, mấy người tỷ phú, triệu phú đâu hết rồi, không thấy gì nhỉ?
A: Where have all the billionaires and millionaires gone? I don't see them anywhere.
B: Where are all those billionaires and millionaires? I don't see any of them.
C: Those billionaires and millionaires, where have they all gone? Can't see any of them, can we?

standard: Vụ gì mà mấy anh đi chơi vui vậy mọi người?
A: What's going on? Why are you guys having so much fun?
B: What's going on that you guys are having so much fun, everyone?
C: What's the occasion that you guys are going out to have fun?

standard: uống nước lọc đi.
A: Drink some plain water.
B: Drink some filtered water.
C: Drink filtered water.

standard: Xóm em có dì Dung nấu ăn ngon tuyệt vời.
A: In my neighborhood, Aunt Dung is an amazing cook.
B: In my neighborhood, there is Aunt Dung who cooks wonderfully.
C: My neighborhood has Aunt Dung who cooks incredibly delicious food.

standard: Ở Nam Đàn không thấy gì.
A: Nothing was seen in Nam Dan.
B: I don't see anything in Nam Dan.
C: I didn't see anything in Nam Dan.

standard: Nó ở đó chứ chạy đâu mà sợ?
A: It's right there, where would it run to?
B: It's there, where could it have gone? No need to be afraid.
C: It's right there, not going anywhere. Why be scared?

standard: Ngày nào còn chở em đi thế này, bây giờ em dư dả, anh thì mịt mù tương lại.
A: The days I used to drive you around like this, and now you're well-off, while my future is bleak.
B: There were days when you still gave me rides like this; now I am well-off, while your future is bleak.
C: Back when he still drove me around like this, now I'm doing well while his future is uncertain.

standard: Chẳng lẽ đi hát ké họ.
A: Should I just go and sing along with them?
B: Am I supposed to just go and sing along with them?
C: Surely I'm not going to go sing by tagging along with them.

standard: Tuỳ khẩu vị, với không phải ở Huế chỗ nào cũng ngon đâu chị ạ.
A: It depends on personal taste, and not every place in Hue is good, you know.
B: It depends on your taste, and not every place in Hue is delicious, ma'am.
C: Depending on taste, not everywhere in Hue is delicious, you know.

standard: Giờ là vui chị ạ. Có cái gì một cái là tập trung về cả đoàn.
A: Things are fun now, sister. Whenever something happens, the whole team gets together.
B: It's fun now. Whenever something happens, the whole group gathers together.
C: Right now is so much fun, sis. There's something that focuses on the whole group.

standard: Con mực này trông to nhỉ.
A: This squid looks big.
B: This squid looks big, doesn't it?
C: This squid looks big, huh.

standard: Món gì cụ này cũng biết hết, tài nhỉ?
A: This old man knows how to cook everything, isn't that amazing?
B: This person knows every single dish, how talented!
C: This elder knows everything, how impressive, right?

standard: Ê, thấy có khi nào lội mưa như vậy đâu? Mới chỉ đội nắng thôi.
A: Hey, I've never seen you walk in the rain like that before. You've only ever been out in the sun.
B: Hey, have you ever seen me wade through the rain like that? I've only ever braved the sun.
C: Hey, when have I ever waded through rain like that? I've only had to walk under the sun.

standard: Cá heo, cá này mà đem kho tiêu cho cay rồi luộc đậu bắp chấm ăn là tuyệt vời
A: Dolphin fish, braised with spicy peppers and served with boiled okra as a dipping sauce, is absolutely delicious.
B: Dolphins; if this fish is braised with pepper to make it spicy and eaten with boiled okra for dipping, it would be wonderful.
C: Dolphin, if we braise this fish with pepper to make it spicy and then boil okra for dipping, it would be amazing

standard: Bạn thân bỏ về nước à?
A: Did your best friend go back to his country?
B: Did your best friend go back to their country?
C: My close friend went back to Vietnam?

standard: Đến mấy ngày sau cổ họng mới hết này nhé
A: It will take several days for the sore throat to go away.
B: It will take a few more days for the throat to recover.
C: It'll take a few more days for my throat to get better.

standard: Rồi nó tức nó phát bóng trúng camera thì lại bảo.
A: Then, when he got angry and hit the ball directly into the camera, they complained.
B: Then it got angry and hit the ball right into the camera, and then they'll complain.
C: So it got frustrated and when it hit the ball and it hit the camera, it started complaining.

standard: ở ta không có Casino cháu nhé
A: We don't have casinos here, my child.
B: We don't have casinos here, dear.
C: We don't have casinos in our country, kid.

standard: Ta bắt taxi. Chứ đâu phải xe đạp xe máy lọc cọc nữa
A: We'll take a taxi. It's not going to be a rickety bicycle or motorbike anymore.
B: I'm taking a taxi. It's not like those clattering bicycles or motorbikes anymore.
C: We'll take a taxi. No more bumpy bike rides for us.

standard: Có ai đưa anh này về chưa thần tượng nhỉ?
A: Has anyone brought this guy home yet, my idol?
B: Has anyone taken this guy home yet, idols?
C: Has anyone taken this guy home yet?

standard: Đói nhăn răng chứ đẹp gì.
A: Being hungry makes you grimace, not look beautiful.
B: I'm starving to death, there's nothing beautiful about it.
C: So hungry my face is all wrinkled - not a good look at all.

standard: Khi đó tôi bày cho học đến 1, 2 giờ sáng đó chứ.
A: Back then, I taught them to study until 1 or 2 in the morning.
B: At that time, I showed them how to do it until 1 or 2 in the morning.
C: Back then, I taught them to study until 1 or 2 in the morning, you know.

standard: Xin lỗi vì sức ép dư luận thôi, chứ không thành tâm đâu
A: I apologize only because of public pressure, not because I'm sincere.
B: Sorry, it's just because of public pressure, not because they are sincere.
C: I'm only apologizing because of public pressure, not because I'm actually sincere.

standard: Mấy anh này ăn gì mà đen thế
A: What did these guys eat to get so dark-skinned?
B: What did these guys eat that made them so dark?
C: What do these guys eat that makes them so dark

standard: mê thật cái sắc đỏ của lon nhé.
A: I really love the red color of the can.
B: I really love the red color of the can.
C: I really love the red color of that can.

standard: E không có gọi hồn tao dậy nhé chúng mày.
A: E, you guys aren't trying to wake me up from the dead.
B: Don't wake me up from the dead, you guys.
C: Don't wake me up, you guys.

standard: lên lịch đi tiếp đi bác nhỉ?
A: Let's schedule another trip, shall we?
B: Shall we schedule the next trip?
C: Shall we schedule to continue, uncle/aunt?

standard: Làm ít cái để anh em đi vợt bươm bướm.
A: Make a few so the guys can go butterfly hunting.
B: Make a few of these so the guys can go butterfly hunting.
C: Made some so we can go butterfly catching.

standard: Ngồi đấy mà bình yên, ngồi tụm lại là nhao nhác như cái chợ.
A: Sitting there is peaceful, but gathering in groups makes it as chaotic as a marketplace.
B: Sit there and act all peaceful, but as soon as you gather together, it's as chaotic as a market.
C: Sitting alone is peaceful, but sitting together is chaotic like a market.

standard: anh em mình hết mình luôn nhé chúng mày
A: Let's give it our all, guys!
B: Let's give it our all, you guys.
C: We'll go all out for sure, you guys!

standard: Tội mấy anh thế.
A: Poor guys.
B: I feel bad for those guys.
C: Poor those guys.

standard: chưa luôn , bận lướt facebook xem đồ ăn này.
A: Not yet, I'm busy browsing Facebook looking at food.
B: Not yet, I'm busy scrolling through Facebook looking at food.
C: Haven't done it yet, busy scrolling Facebook looking at this food.

standard: Sáng thức dậy, nhìn trời nắng đẹp.
A: Woke up this morning and saw the beautiful sunny sky.
B: I woke up this morning and saw that the weather was sunny and beautiful.
C: I woke up in the morning and saw beautiful sunny weather.

standard: vậy là ngày nào cũng được giải trí hết vậy à, quá đã
A: So, we get to have fun every day? That's awesome!
B: So that means I get to be entertained every single day, how awesome.
C: So I get to be entertained every single day like that, so awesome!

standard: em có biết gì đâu, thấy buồn chị nhỉ.
A: I didn't know anything, it's sad, isn't it, sister?
B: I don't know anything, it's sad, isn't it?
C: I wouldn't know anything about it, but it does seem sad, doesn't it?

standard: Nghỉ hè chưa chán!
A: Summer vacation is not boring yet!
B: I'm not tired of summer break yet!
C: Summer vacation hasn't gotten boring yet!

standard: Bữa nay không cần ra đê sông Lam nữa, chèo sup sang chỗ chúng tôi chơi đi.
A: No need to go to the Lam River dike today, just paddle your SUP over to our place to hang out.
B: Today you don't need to go to the Lam River dike anymore, just paddle your SUP over to our place to hang out.
C: Today you don't need to go to the Lam River embankment anymore, just paddle your SUP over here and hang out with us.

standard: Sao mà cũng ở Nghệ An mà chưa thấy anh này khi nào
A: How come I've never seen this guy before, even though he's also from Nghe An?
B: How is it that we're both in Nghe An, yet I've never seen this guy before?
C: How come I've never seen this guy even though we're both in Nghệ An

standard: Cá heo kho tiêu ngon tuyệt vời.
A: Braised dolphin fish with pepper is absolutely delicious.
B: The pepper-braised dolphin is absolutely delicious.
C: Dolphin fish braised with black pepper is absolutely delicious.

standard: Mày lọt cỡ khe nào, nói tao nghe.
A: Tell me how big of a gap you fit through.
B: Which size gap do you fit into? Tell me.
C: What size gap can you squeeze through, tell me.

standard: có mà chở đi hít khói xe, chút về nhà xem cho nhàn
A: It'll just be a waste of time breathing in exhaust fumes; it'll be more relaxing to watch it at home later.
B: I'll just take you to breathe in exhaust fumes; it's easier to just go home and watch it in a bit.
C: Instead of taking me out to breathe in car exhaust, let's go home and watch something relaxing instead.

standard: Về chủ yếu với mẹ với em chứ có phải về chơi với bạn đâu cậu.
A: I'm mainly going home to see my mom and my sister, not to hang out with friends.
B: I'm coming back mainly for my mother and younger sibling, not to hang out with friends.
C: I'm mainly going home to be with my mom and my younger sibling, not to hang out with friends, you know.

standard: Món khoái khẩu nhất Hà Tĩnh của cô đây rồi.
A: This is her favorite dish in Ha Tinh.
B: Here is her favorite Ha Tinh dish.
C: Here's my most favorite Ha Tinh dish.

standard: Có điều về nửa đêm có đâu mà đi nhỉ?
A: But where would I go at midnight?
B: But where is there to go in the middle of the night?
C: There's just something about midnight - there's nowhere to go anyway, is there?

standard: Em bò đã không biết rồi, chứ nói gì đến bứt cỏ.
A: The cow didn't know how to do it, let alone pull up grass.
B: I didn't even know about the cow, let alone pulling up the grass.
C: If the one who crawls doesn't even know, how could they pull grass?

standard: Sợ cái khung nó cấn cái đó, nó gãy.
A: I'm afraid the frame might press against that and break.
B: I'm afraid the frame will press against that and it will break.
C: I'm afraid the frame might press against that and break.

standard: Cái này cứu bóng mới chuẩn.
A: This is the perfect way to save the ball.
B: This is the right way to save the ball.
C: This one saves balls properly.

standard: Mưa gió vậy mà không ai tắt dùm mấy cái quạt, để làm gì tốn điện?
A: Despite the stormy weather, why doesn't anyone turn off the fans? What's the point of leaving them on if it wastes electricity?
B: It's raining and windy like this, yet no one turned off the fans; why leave them on and waste electricity?
C: It's raining and windy like this, yet no one turned off those fans for me. What's the point of wasting electricity?

standard: chơi hết mình để biết dân miền Tây là sao này.
A: Play hard to find out what the people of the Mekong Delta are like.
B: Play with all my heart to see what people from the West are like.
C: Going all out to find out what people from the Western region are all about.

standard: Lấy cái ly uống nước ở đâu vậy?
A: Where can I get a drinking glass?
B: Where can I get a glass of water?
C: Where can I find the drinking glass?

standard: Hôm nào mấy anh ấy về bên đấy rủ qua Việt Nam du lịch hết luôn.
A: Whenever those guys go back there, they'll all invite me to come to Vietnam for a vacation.
B: Whenever those guys go back there, invite them all to come travel in Vietnam.
C: Whenever those guys go back to that place, have them invite us over to Vietnam for a trip together, all of us.

standard: Tôi ở Ba Đồn tôi không biết.
A: I live in Ba Don, so I don't know.
B: I am in Ba Don, so I don't know.
C: I'm in Ba Đồn, I don't know.

standard: Sau này nhà có xe mà không chạy có bị phạt không nhỉ?
A: Will we be fined if we own a car but don't use it?
B: In the future, if my family has a car but doesn't drive it, will I be fined?
C: I wonder if households that own vehicles but don't drive them will be fined in the future?

standard: Lo xuống đi đã. Trên này lịch khi nào cũng rảnh.
A: Come down first. My schedule is always free up here.
B: Just go down first. My schedule up here is always free.
C: Just go down first. I'm always free up here.

standard: Ném vậy mà đắp chiếu nghe sợ chị nhỉ. Nhà kia cho em nhìn ảnh bà mà thương.
A: Throwing her away and then covering her with a sheet sounds scary, doesn't it? That family showed me a picture of their grandmother and I felt so sorry for her.
B: Throwing it away like that while being covered with a mat sounds scary, doesn't it, sister? Looking at the photos of the lady from that other house makes me feel pity.
C: Throwing like that and covering it with a mat sounds scary, doesn't it. The people over there showed me a picture of the old lady and it made me feel sad.

standard: Cố gắng lên tất cả vì miền Bắc thân yêu, bọn chúng ăn ốc nói mò thì biết gì đâu.
A: Let's all try our best for our beloved North; those guys who talk nonsense know nothing.
B: Keep fighting, everyone, for our beloved North; they are just talking nonsense, so they don't know anything.
C: Keep it up, everyone, for our beloved North. They're just talking nonsense anyway, what do they know.

standard: Xã nào mà có sáu chị em gái, mấy cô chỉ được cái nói bậy, không ai nghe ai đâu
A: In a village with six sisters, all they do is talk nonsense; nobody listens to anyone else.
B: Which commune has six sisters, where the ladies only know how to talk nonsense and no one listens to anyone else?
C: Which village would have six sisters - those women only know how to talk nonsense, and no one listens to anyone.

standard: Mình nhìn mà xấu hổ đấy trời.
A: I'm so embarrassed looking at this.
B: I'm so embarrassed just looking at it, oh my god.
C: I'm so embarrassed looking at this, goodness.

standard: Các cụ gánh gãy cả lưng mà.
A: The old folks carried those loads until their backs ached.
B: The elders are breaking their backs carrying it.
C: The elders carried (things) until their backs broke.

standard: Vậy chắc nhà tao nghèo chúng mày ơi chúng mày.
A: So, I guess my family is poor, you guys.
B: So my family must be poor, you guys.
C: So my family must be poor, you guys.

standard: Sao không phải là chị mà là 2 em nhỉ?
A: Why not my sister, but my two younger siblings?
B: Why isn't it you, but the two of them instead?
C: Why is it not the older sister but rather 2 younger ones?

standard: Ngon lắm nhé, cháo lá dứa Long Xuyên ăn là tuyệt vời.
A: It's delicious! Long Xuyên pandan leaf porridge is amazing.
B: It's very delicious; Long Xuyen pandan leaf porridge is wonderful to eat.
C: So delicious, pandan leaf congee from Long Xuyên is absolutely amazing.

standard: Ế nấm rồi. Ai cứu em với chứ ăn không hết rồi
A: I'm out of mushrooms! Someone please save me, I can't eat them all!
B: I have leftover mushrooms. Someone please save me, I can't eat them all.
C: I have so many mushrooms left. Someone help me, I can't finish eating them all.

standard: bài ca muôn thuở, nói thôi chứ có thực hiện đâu
A: It's the same old story, all talk, no action.
B: The same old story; they just talk but never actually do it.
C: Same old empty promises - just talking but never actually following through

standard: Xế chiều nhìn thấy món này là ruột gan bất ổn ngay
A: Seeing this dish in the afternoon immediately makes my stomach churn.
B: Seeing this dish in the late afternoon makes my stomach feel restless immediately.
C: Late afternoon, seeing this dish just makes me feel sick right away

standard: Chỉ có nhà Tuấn đại gia mới đẳng cấp vậy thôi.
A: Only the wealthy Tuan family could be that classy.
B: Only the wealthy Tuan's family can be that classy.
C: Only Tuấn the rich guy is that classy, that's all.
